claim: Hand A text content is independent of the illustrated plant identity. Under the content-dependent alternative, folios depicting same-family plants should show significantly higher pairwise lexical similarity than folios depicting different-family plants. Under the independence hypothesis, text similarity is uncorrelated with botanical relationships and Hand A is generic text re-used across illustrations rather than plant-specific description.
evidence_for: H-BV-SYLLABLE-LEVEL-01 found Hand-A plant folio token counts in narrow range (45-147, sd 19) independent of Sherwood proxy for plant prominence — consistent with generic text
Three consecutive character-map refutations (Schechter, Pagel expanded, syllable) are all consistent with 'Hand A is structured text but not plant-specific'
If text is plant-specific, natural-language decoders should eventually work; if generic, no character-map will produce plant-aligned content
evidence_against: H-BV-PLANT-01 confirmed _.oii marker concentrates on plant folios — some plant-specific signal is detectable
Jensen's independent TF-IDF clustering placed plant folios in distinct attractors — suggests plant-specific vocabulary differentiation
Genus/family-level groups are small in the Sherwood sample (biggest family asteraceae has 19 folios), limiting statistical power
test: LOCKED: (1) For each Hand-A plant folio, compute word-frequency vector. (2) Compute pairwise cosine similarity between every pair of Hand-A plant folios. (3) Group folios by plant family using the genus->family map from scripts/perun_visual_key_test.py (covers common Mediterranean genera). (4) Compute mean within-family cosine vs mean cross-family cosine. (5) Permutation test: shuffle folio-family labels 1000 times, count fraction of shuffles where within-family mean exceeds observed. (6) Secondary: Pearson r between folio-pair text cosine similarity and folio-pair medicinal_use text Jaccard.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED PLANT-SPECIFIC if (a) within-family cosine > cross-family cosine AND permutation-test p < 0.05. Then H-BV-CONTENT-INDEPENDENCE is REFUTED. CONFIRMED INDEPENDENT if within ~= cross (difference < 0.01) AND permutation p > 0.20 AND secondary medicinal-correlation r < 0.1. MARGINAL otherwise.

tests_run.details: n=83 Hand-A plant folios with known family. Within-family mean cosine 0.2407, cross-family 0.2622, diff -0.0214 (within LESS similar than cross, opposite of plant-specific direction). Permutation p=0.856 (not significant in plant-specific direction). Medicinal-use correlation r=-0.041. Under locked rule: |diff|=0.021 exceeds 0.01 threshold so INDEPENDENCE-CONFIRMED path fails; within<cross so PLANT-SPECIFIC path also fails -> MARGINAL. Substantive reading: text is directionally content-independent. No botanical-relationship -> text-similarity link.

reasoning: High-stakes test. If plant-specific, Brain-V continues decoding; if independent, reframes everything. Five character-map refutations already point toward the independence hypothesis.
